articles.title: Establishing Crew Exposure Limits of Martian Dust
articles.summary: Background Human exploration of Mars will expose crews to a persistent, fine particulate environment whose physicochemical properties and health implications remain only partly understood. Because no samples of authentic Martian airborne dust have been returned to Earth, NASA must rely on lunar dust toxicology, Martian regolith simulants, and extensive rover/lander geochemical and mineralogical datasets to […]

articles.title: A New Compact Instrument Enables High-Fidelity Measurements of Energetic Particles on CubeSats
articles.summary: A team of NASA-sponsored scientists and engineers has developed a novel approach to observing high-energy particles in the near-Earth space environment, incorporating miniaturized sensors into a compact, multi-view particle detection instrument unlike any before it. Built for NASA’s Relativistic Electron Atmospheric Loss (REAL) CubeSat mission, the innovative instrument (also called REAL) enables more complete measurements […]

articles.title: UK and Florida commit $400,000 to joint space projects
articles.summary:
The British government and Florida’s space-focused economic development agency have each committed $200,000 to support joint research, innovation and commercialization projects.
The post UK and Florida commit $400,000 to joint space projects appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: German component supplier deltaVision raises 10.2 million euros
articles.summary:
German spacecraft component developer deltaVision has raised 10.2 million euros ($11.6 million) to expand production of valves and related equipment, with a focus on enabling in-space refueling.
The post German component supplier deltaVision raises 10.2 million euros appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch novel geosynchronous robotic servicing satellite on decade-long mission
articles.summary: Northrop Grumman’s Mission Robotic Vehicle features the Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) payload from the U.S. Naval Research Laboratory and DARPA. Liftoff from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled during a four-hour window that opens at 5:15 p.m. EDT (2115 UTC).

articles.title: ExoMars ramps put up with cold, shaking and vacuum

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX tries again to launch Starlink mission from California following abort
articles.summary: The Starlink 17-39 mission is SpaceX’s 85th Falcon 9 rocket launch of the year. Liftoff from pad 4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base is scheduled for 7:43 a.m. PDT (10:43 a.m. EDT / 1443 UTC).

articles.title: KBR organizes defense technology business to pursue Golden Dome work
articles.summary:
Company points to digital engineering, space tracking and missile-defense experience 
The post KBR organizes defense technology business to pursue Golden Dome work appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: IHI explores Kuva hyperspectral satellites for Japan’s multi-sensor constellation
articles.summary:
Japan’s IHI is considering adding next-generation hyperspectral satellites from Finnish startup Kuva Space to its planned 100-strong, multi-sensor sovereign Earth observation constellation.
The post IHI explores Kuva hyperspectral satellites for Japan’s multi-sensor constellation appeared first on SpaceNews.
